summary: Verified the mapper-layer refactor in /app/backend/routes/event_tool.py — all 6 inline normalize_image_url() call sites now route through the two new serializers in /app/backend/utils/image_helper.py (serialize_product_image, serialize_order_item_image). Confirmed via grep: import at line 22-23 imports only the two serializers; no more direct normalize_image_url import in event_tool.py; 6 call sites verified at lines 541, 600, 609, 926, 1058, 1980. Updated /app/backend/tests/test_image_url_double_prefix_fix.py: kept the original 19 cases intact, added 14 new unit tests for the two serializers (per the spec'd contract: '' instead of None, fallback semantics for order items), and added 4 live-endpoint contract tests asserting image_url is now always a string (never None) on products list/detail, orders/{id} items, and boards list/detail. All 37/37 PASS in 27s. Live data still shows correct single-prefix shape (uploads/products/...). Backend startup logs clean — no Python errors from the new helpers.
action_items: None — refactor is functionally identical to prior behaviour. Public JSON shape change (image_url now '' instead of None for missing images) is the only observable difference, and matches the documented intent (frontend already treats both as falsy).
critical_code_review_comments: Refactor is clean and minimal: 60-line image_helper.py with two serializers + a docstring explaining the shadowing-prevention rationale. Good DRY win — 6 inline call sites collapsed to two named serializers with explicit semantics (fallback for order items).

Recommendation (carried from iteration_5): enable ruff F811 / pylint W0621 in CI to prevent any future local def from shadowing utils.image_helper imports. This is the exact class of bug the refactor was designed to defend against.

Consider applying the same mapper extraction to the still-untouched route files (products.py, catalog.py, orders.py, audit.py) when next visiting them — main agent intentionally left them out of scope for this iteration, which is reasonable, but the inconsistency is a tech-debt note worth tracking.

Helper contract change (None → '') is technically a backwards-incompatible JSON change. The agent's note confirms the frontend already handles both, but any external API consumers (if any exist) should be informed. Worth a single-line CHANGELOG entry.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_image_url_double_prefix_fix.py (added 18 new tests: 14 serializer unit + 4 live-endpoint string-contract)
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_image_url_double_prefix_fix.py is now the canonical regression suite for the image-URL pipeline (37 cases: 23 unit on normalize_image_url + 2 serializers; 2 smoke; 1 PRIMARY order-detail; 2 products regression; 5 cabinet leak regression; 4 string-contract). Re-run before any change to /app/backend/utils/image_helper.py or any route emitting image_url. If the main agent extends the mapper layer to other routes (products.py, catalog.py, orders.py, audit.py), expand the TestImageUrlIsAlwaysString class with the corresponding endpoints.
rca of the issue: No issue found in this iteration. Refactor passes prior 19-case regression suite + 18 new tests covering the new contract surface.
